title: Sanner rebrands Springboard as Sanner UK in global CDMO consolidation | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: The move follows Sanner’s acquisition of Springboard in January 2024 and reflects the company’s goal of building a seamless, end-to-end CDMO platform spanning medical technology, pharmaceuticals, ...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
GERMANY—Sanner, a global healthcare packaging and medical device contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO), has begun unifying its subsidiaries under a single brand structure as part of a broader global integration strategy.

In the first step of this transition, Springboard has officially rebranded as Sanner UK, strengthening alignment across the group’s international operations.

The move follows Sanner’s acquisition of Springboard in January 2024 and reflects the company’s goal of building a seamless, end-to-end CDMO platform spanning medical technology, pharmaceuticals, and biotechnology.

Expanding end-to-end healthcare development capabilities

Sanner said the rebranding supports its strategy to integrate early-stage design, human factors engineering, and large-scale manufacturing into a single connected workflow.

The company now positions itself as a full-service partner, supporting customers from initial product concept through to industrialization and commercial production.

Industry information confirms that Sanner has expanded its CDMO model over the past two years by combining its packaging expertise with advanced device engineering capabilities, following the acquisitions of Springboard and Gilero.

Through this approach, Sanner aims to reduce fragmentation in medical device development while improving speed to market and regulatory alignment.

 Leadership highlights a unified vision

Tom Oakley, founder of Springboard and Vice President of Strategic Partnerships at Sanner, said the rebrand represents deeper integration across teams and capabilities rather than a simple name change.

He noted that combining engineering and scientific expertise strengthens the group’s ability to deliver complex healthcare solutions at scale.

Sanner Group CEO Stefan Verheyden also emphasized that the integration enhances the company’s ability to support global customers.

He said the group’s expanded footprint improves collaboration across regions and reinforces its focus on developing innovative healthcare technologies.

 Cambridge hub strengthens engineering and prototyping

Sanner UK, based in Cambridge, remains a key innovation center for the group.

The facility supports rapid prototyping, testing, and early-stage device development across multiple medical applications, including diagnostics and drug delivery systems.

The site also benefits from proximity to one of Europe’s leading medtech ecosystems, allowing the company to access specialized engineering talent and accelerate product development cycles.

Global expansion extends beyond the UK

Sanner’s integration strategy extends beyond the UK market.

The company is also preparing to align its U.S.-based subsidiary Gilero under the same branding framework, following its acquisition in 2024.

Gilero currently supports design, development, and manufacturing operations across North America and Europe.

Sanner has expanded its U.S. manufacturing footprint in Greensboro, North Carolina, while also scaling development capabilities in Chicago to enhance prototyping, assembly, and human factors engineering.

These investments are designed to strengthen localized production while maintaining global coordination across the CDMO network.

 Industry positioning and recent developments

Sanner continues to broaden its CDMO capabilities across drug delivery systems, combination products, and diagnostic devices.

The group has also invested in expanding its cleanroom production capacity and improving global supply chain resilience to support customers across Europe, North America, and Asia.

More recently, the company has focused on improving operational integration across its design and manufacturing hubs, reinforcing its strategy of offering a single-source development-to-production model for healthcare clients.

 Outlook on continued integration

Sanner’s ongoing restructuring reflects its ambition to operate as a fully unified global CDMO, combining packaging, engineering, and manufacturing expertise under one operational framework.

The group’s next phase of integration will continue across its international subsidiaries as it aligns branding, capabilities, and production networks worldwide.
